title: Use `command -v` rather than `which`
body: `which` is [non-portable](https://twitter.com/whitequark/status/1168670168173559808), requiring an additional package installation on some systems, as I ran into trying to get the NixOS package to run yosys tests; the most common alternative, `type -p` is [POSIX-portable in terms of return value, but not output](https://twitter.com/dev_console/status/1168672291271692288). That doesn't matter particularly here, but I've replaced it with `command -v`, which is [portable in return value and output](https://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/utilities/command.html).
